name: rquickjs
description: High-level Rust bindings to the QuickJS-NG JavaScript engine with ES2020 support

implementation.comment: Rust bindings wrapping the QuickJS-NG C library

paradigm.comment: Supports all JavaScript paradigms including prototype-based OOP

pattern-matching.comment: JavaScript has destructuring but no true pattern matching

error-handling.comment: JavaScript uses try/catch/finally/throw for error handling

async-await.comment: Full async/await support with ES6 Promises converted to Rust futures

iterators-generators.comment: Supports ES6 iterators, generators, and async generators

module-system.comment: ES6 modules with custom resolvers and loaders via loader feature

standard-library.comment: Full ES2020 built-ins: Object, Array, Promise, BigInt, Proxy, etc.

execution-strategy.comment: QuickJS compiles JavaScript to bytecode for execution

compilation-model.comment: Supports runtime eval and precompiled bytecode embedding via macro

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be re-evaluated at runtime

debugging-support.comment: No built-in debugger or breakpoint support found in documentation

startup-time.comment: Startup under 300 microseconds for complete runtime lifecycle

execution-speed.comment: QuickJS runs 75,000 ECMAScript tests in ~100 seconds on single core; no JIT but well-optimized bytecode

memory-overhead.comment: 210 KiB x86 code for hello world; designed for embedding in resource-constrained environments

garbage-collection.comment: Reference counting with cycle removal for deterministic behavior

pure-rust.comment: Bindings to QuickJS-NG C library via rquickjs-sys

api-safety.comment: Safe high-level wrapper; unsafe contained in FFI layer

idiomatic-api.comment: 100% documented; derive macros for FromJs/IntoJs, Result error handling, builder patterns, similar to rlua

serde-integration.comment: Via rquickjs-serde crate for serialization between Rust and JavaScript values

async-compatible.comment: AsyncRuntime and AsyncContext with futures-aware locks; ES6 Promises convert to Rust futures bidirectionally

function-registration.comment: Derive macros for FromJs/IntoJs traits plus builder API for function registration

type-mapping.comment: IntoJs/FromJs traits for automatic conversion; serde support via rquickjs-serde

no-std-compatible.comment: QuickJS requires libc; not designed for no_std environments

wasm-compatible.comment: Pre-generated bindings for wasm32-wasip1 and wasm32-wasip2 targets

blank-slate-mode.comment: Starts with full ECMAScript standard library; cannot easily remove built-ins

memory-sandboxing.comment: JavaScript VM prevents direct host memory access

cpu-limits.comment: set_interrupt_handler() can raise uncatchable exception to stop execution

memory-limits.comment: set_memory_limit() caps total runtime memory; set_max_stack_size() limits stack (default 256KB)

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O; only what host explicitly exposes

capability-security.comment: Standard JavaScript environment without fine-grained capability controls

default-deny.comment: Full JavaScript standard library available by default

share-data.comment: Data converted through FromJs/IntoJs traits; no direct memory sharing

custom-types.comment: ES6 class support with Rust data type representation

operator-overloading.comment: JavaScript doesn't support operator overloading; only valueOf/Symbol.toPrimitive

method-syntax.comment: JavaScript objects support method definition and dot-notation calls

callbacks-events.comment: JavaScript functions as callbacks; Promise hooks for tracking

package-manager.comment: No dedicated package ecosystem; uses ES modules with custom loaders

editor-support.comment: Uses standard JavaScript tooling; VS Code and other JS editors work

example-projects.comment: AWS LLRT uses rquickjs; wasm-rquickjs for WASM components

documentation-quality.comment: 100% documented according to docs.rs; comprehensive API docs with examples

bus-factor.comment: 45 contributors with 2-3 main maintainers (Mees Delzenne, Kayo Phoenix)

maturity.comment: Described as 'feature complete, mostly stable and ready to use'

primary-use-cases.comment: Lightweight JavaScript embedding; AWS LLRT serverless runtime uses it
